summary: Linked the fighter ftcolanim object by making a small codegen-oriented source tweak in ftCo_800BFFD0, updating GALE01 symbol scopes/local labels, and flipping melee/ft/ftcolanim.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The automated report confirmed 1 new match: main/melee/ft/ftcolanim ftCo_800BFFD0 improved from 97.41% to 100.00%, with linked code +2028 bytes and linked data +80 bytes.

key_files.role: Removed the unused ft/types.h include and changed ftCo_800BFFD0's arg1 subtraction/call argument expression to reach a match.
Adjusted data, bss, sdata, and sdata2 symbol names/scopes, including local @NNN literals and ...data.0/...bss.0 labels, for the linked object.

what_changed: configure.py now builds/links melee/ft/ftcolanim.c as Matching instead of NonMatching.
ftCo_800BFFD0 now declares long tmp, mutates arg1 with arg1 -= (tmp = 0x7B), and passes the adjusted arg1 to lb_800144C8 instead of using a separate s32 temp_r7.

config/GALE01/symbols.txt changed several anonymous constants/literals from global or unnamed-scope entries to scope:local entries, including @162, @334, @335, @336, @343, and multiple @NNN sdata2 floats/doubles.
Some symbols previously named as ftCo address globals were restored to anonymous local literal names, including ftCo_803C6AE8 to @248, ftCo_803C6B08 to @249, and ftCo_804D3C10 to @250.
Added local section labels ...data.0 at .data:0x803BAB60 and ...bss.0 at .bss:0x80433318.
smart_moves: Kept the matching source change very small and isolated to ftCo_800BFFD0 before flipping the whole object to Matching.
Used the exact expression shape arg1 -= (tmp = 0x7B) to alter compiler output without changing the apparent high-level behavior of the threshold/subtraction path.
Handled symbol-map cleanup in the same PR as the link flip, avoiding a code-only match that would still leave data/linkage mismatches.
Converted local anonymous strings/floats/doubles back to @NNN scope:local symbols instead of leaving them as misleading exported ftCo_ address globals.
Relied on the automated decomp.dev report to verify both function-level matching and object-level linked code/data gains.
decomp_lessons: For final-byte fighter matches, a seemingly awkward C expression can be useful when it matches compiler register/immediate behavior better than a cleaner temporary variable.
When linking a previously NonMatching object, audit symbols.txt for local-vs-global scope; anonymous literals and constants often need @NNN scope:local entries.
Do not assume module-prefixed address names such as ftCo_803C6AE8 are true globals; this PR shows some were actually local anonymous strings.
Section-local labels like ...data.0 and ...bss.0 may be needed alongside object symbols when matching linked data layout.
A configure.py Matching flip should be paired with evidence from match/link metrics, not just source-level confidence.
naming_conventions: Anonymous compiler literals/constants use @NNN names with scope:local, including strings, sjis strings, floats, and doubles.
Local section labels use ellipsis-style names such as ...data.0 and ...bss.0 with type:label scope:local.
Fighter common functions keep the ftCo_ prefix, as in ftCo_800BFFAC and ftCo_800BFFD0.
Fighter globals referenced by ftcolanim use Fighter_ address names such as Fighter_804D6538 and Fighter_804D653C.
Unidentified true globals can remain module/address named, but local literals should not be promoted to ftCo_ address globals without evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace a clean separate temporary, s32 temp_r7 = arg1 - 0x7B, with mutation of the parameter, arg1 -= (tmp = 0x7B), when disassembly requires that expression/register shape.
Introducing an otherwise minimal long tmp assignment inside an expression can be a targeted codegen nudge; use only with assembly verification.
Pass the mutated parameter directly to the callee when the target assembly does not preserve a separate named temporary.
Synchronize local symbol scope fixes across .data, .sdata, .sdata2, and .bss before marking the object Matching.
After a one-function final match, update configure.py so the object contributes to linked code/data progress.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 report showed matched code 61.58% (+164 bytes), linked code 33.79% (+2028 bytes), linked data 32.33% (+80 bytes), and 1 new match: main/melee/ft/ftcolanim ftCo_800BFFD0 from 97.41% to 100.00%.
No human review comments or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find other fighter objects still marked Object(NonMatching) near ftcolanim.c in configure.py.
Search for remaining ftCo_ address-named symbols that are actually local @NNN string or float literals.
Look for other final-byte matches using dummy long temporaries or assignment expressions like tmp = constant.
Audit symbols.txt entries with anonymous @NNN names still marked scope:global.
Compare later ftmaterial or ftdevice linking PRs for similar symbol-scope cleanup patterns.
